metadata.title: Why parallelism is a prompt problem, not a code problem
metadata.purpose: Sub-agents spawn when the model emits `Task` calls; multiple `Task` calls in ONE model turn are run concurrently by the SDK, so "together or one at a time" is a prompt strategy, not application logic.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (sequential vs. single-burst timeline with Task arrows), raster (before/after prompt diff, one added phrase)
metadata.prerequisites: what sub-agents are, the Task tool
metadata.exclusions: async Python internals, thread safety, Task implementation, retry/rate-limit behavior
metadata.visual_object: Two turn-timelines side by side — five one-Task turns vs. one five-Task turn — with a wall-clock bar on each.
metadata.hook: You reach for `asyncio.gather()` to run sub-agents in parallel; instead you edit one line of the system prompt and the SDK parallelizes for you.

beats.narration_text: Briefing five companies. "Research each in turn" runs five sequential turns; "spawn one researcher per company in the same turn" runs all five at once — identical SDK code, one changed phrase.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Needing parallel execution should predict writing concurrent code; here the code never changes and only the prompt does — why does the system prompt decide sequential vs. parallel?
You reach for `asyncio.gather()` to run sub-agents in parallel; instead you edit one line of the system prompt and the SDK parallelizes for you.

Sub-agents spawn when the model emits `Task` calls; multiple `Task` calls in ONE model turn are run concurrently by the SDK, so "together or one at a time" is a prompt strategy, not application logic.
beats.visual: Two turn-timelines side by side — five one-Task turns vs. one five-Task turn — with a wall-clock bar on each.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Five briefings — sequential ~25s across five round-trips, parallel ~6s in one turn; the only diff is "in the same turn" in the prompt.

Sub-agents spawn when the model emits `Task` calls; multiple `Task` calls in ONE model turn are run concurrently by the SDK, so "together or one at a time" is a prompt strategy, not application logic.
